title: stream_header_len not reset on header parsing failure
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/meshcore/adapter.c
Line: 1143-1150

When a frame header is parsed with invalid length (0 or > MAX_SERIAL_PAYLOAD), the code resets stream_expected but not stream_header_len:
```c
adapter->stream_expected =
    sys_get_le16(&adapter->stream_header[1]);
adapter->stream_header_len = 0U;  // This is set...
adapter->stream_len = 0U;
if (adapter->stream_expected == 0U ||
    adapter->stream_expected >
    CONFIG_LICHEN_MESHCORE_MAX_SERIAL_PAYLOAD) {
    adapter->stats.malformed_count++;
    adapter->stream_expected = 0U;  // ...but here stream_header_len is already 0
    continue;
}
```

Actually, looking more carefully: stream_header_len IS reset to 0 at line 1142 before the check. This is correct. The reset happens before the validity check. This is NOT a bug.

Actually wait, let me re-read the flow more carefully. The sequence is:
1. Line 1135: stream_header[stream_header_len++] = byte
2. Line 1136-1138: if < sizeof(stream_header), continue
3. Line 1140-1142: parse expected length, reset header_len to 0
4. Line 1143-1149: if invalid, reset expected and continue

So stream_header_len is always 0 when we reach line 1143. This is correct.

Let me cancel this bead and look for other issues.
